title: Will President Trump send ICE agents to Boston’s Logan Airport?
author: Molly Farrar
bullets: President Trump plans to send ICE agents to airports if DHS funding is not restored by Democrats.
Logan Airport has mostly avoided delays, with Massport reporting no impact from the government shutdown so far.
AFGE Local 2617 President Mike Gayzagian states it's unclear if ICE will be deployed to New England airports.

title: Is Bruins goalie Jeremy Swayman a legitimate Vezina candidate? The numbers speak for themselves.
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Jeremy Swayman made 42 saves in a 4-2 victory over the Detroit Red Wings, boosting Bruins' playoff odds.
Swayman ranks second in the NHL in goals saved above expected at 27.1, behind Washington's Logan Thompson.
Despite a .908 save percentage, Swayman faces high-danger shots frequently, making him a Vezina Trophy contender.

title: Celtics legend Paul Pierce continues to make a case for Jaylen Brown as NBA MVP
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Paul Pierce advocates for Jaylen Brown as an NBA MVP candidate, citing his outstanding performance with Boston Celtics.
Brown averages 28.5 points, 7.0 rebounds, and 5.2 assists per game, leading Celtics to second place in Eastern Conference.
Pierce attributes lack of individual recognition for Celtics players to Boston's longstanding sports success creating resentment.

title: Plymouth police officer on paid leave amid criminal investigation
author: Molly Farrar
bullets: Officer Samantha Pelrine is on paid leave amid a criminal investigation into her off-duty conduct by Massachusetts State Police.
The Plymouth Police Department is performing an internal investigation and emphasizes accountability and transparency with the community.
The Plymouth County District Attorney's Office and State Police are withholding further details during the ongoing investigation.

title: MLB writer predicts Roman Anthony will win AL MVP this season
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Eno Sarris of The Athletic predicts Roman Anthony will win the AL MVP in the 2026 MLB season.
Anthony impressed in the 2026 World Baseball Classic, hitting .280 with two home runs and a team-leading seven RBI.
In his rookie season, Anthony played 71 games with a .292 average, eight home runs, and 32 RBI for the Red Sox.

title: Pedestrian killed in Roxbury hit-and-run
author: Carson Lyle
bullets: A pedestrian died in a hit-and-run on Southampton and Bradston streets in Roxbury, Boston police reported.
The driver fled the scene after the crash; the vehicle involved was found later, according to police.
No arrests have been made yet, and the investigation into the hit-and-run is ongoing, police stated.

title: Here’s what meteorologists are saying about chances for snow through Monday
author: Jack Pickell
bullets: National Weather Service predicts a coating to 2 inches of wet snow near I-90 by Monday night.
Vicki Graf forecasts minor snow accumulation, with some areas receiving up to 2 inches in steadier bands.
Rain likely switches to wet snow and sleet in northern MA, CT, RI, and parts of SE MA early Monday.

title: Mass. police officer arrested for OUI, department says
author: Carson Lyle
bullets: Norwood officer Shawn Willman was arrested for operating a vehicle under the influence of alcohol.
Willman, off duty at the time, has been placed on administrative leave pending the case's outcome.
An internal affairs investigation into Willman's arrest is underway by Norwood Police Department.

title: Police: Mass. man allegedly stabbed friend during fight over woman at Miami Beach bachelor party
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: Olajuwon Shawn Dickerson, 32, was arrested for allegedly stabbing his friend Brian Gordon at a Miami Beach bachelor party.
The altercation reportedly began over women met at bars, escalating into a physical confrontation in an SUV.
Both men sustained injuries; Dickerson was charged with aggravated battery and held without bond in Florida.

title: 4 takeaways from the Revolution’s 3-1 loss to St. Louis City SC
author: Hayden Bird
bullets: New England Revolution lost 3-1 to St. Louis City SC, marking their third defeat in four games this season.
Ilay Feingold scored first for New England, but St. Louis City SC responded with three unanswered goals.
New England forward Leo Campana exited with an injury in the 80th minute; the extent is yet unknown.
